touches.ai_summary: Noah Desai submitted an inbound request via the website expressing interest in CRM automation capabilities. He mentioned they're currently evaluating solutions to help their sales team work more efficiently.
next_steps: Rep to follow up with qualification call scheduling

touches.ai_summary: Rep sent follow-up email thanking Noah for the inbound request and proposing times for an introductory qualification call. Included brief overview of CRM automation capabilities and case study link.

Brief qualification call with Noah to understand VortexDynamics' current CRM setup and automation needs. Noah explained they have ~20 sales reps manually logging activities and want to reduce admin burden. He mentioned they're also looking at Gong.

questions_asked.question: What's your current process for logging sales activities?
questions_asked.answer: Mostly manual entry in Salesforce. Reps spend 1-2 hours daily on admin work.

questions_asked.question: How does your solution compare to Gong?
questions_asked.answer: Rep explained overlap in call recording but emphasized broader CRM automation beyond just calls.

next_steps: Rep to send feature comparison doc

touches.ai_summary: Rep sent follow-up email with feature comparison highlighting CRM automation breadth vs. Gong's call-focused approach. Included ROI calculator and proposed next call agenda.

Second qualification call diving deeper into VortexDynamics' tech stack and integration requirements. Noah confirmed Salesforce CRM and interest in email automation alongside call recording. Budget range discussed (~$15-20K).

questions_asked.question: What integrations are must-haves for you?
questions_asked.answer: Salesforce definitely, Gmail/Outlook for email logging, ideally Slack.

questions_asked.question: What's the typical implementation timeline?
questions_asked.answer: Rep outlined 2-4 week standard implementation with technical support.

touches.ai_summary: Rep sent discovery summary email recapping VortexDynamics' requirements and proposed demo agenda focused on CRM automation workflow, email/call logging, and Salesforce integration. Requested Noah invite technical stakeholders.

questions_asked.question: Who else should we involve from your team for the technical evaluation?
questions_asked.answer: Noah mentioned bringing in Diego Williams from implementation team.

touches.ai_summary: Follow-up email with demo scheduling link and pre-demo questionnaire to customize the presentation. Rep also shared customer reference in similar SaaS/tech vertical.
Technical evaluation meeting originally scheduled but rescheduled by Noah due to conflicting exec meeting. Brief exchange to find new time later in the week.

First demo session focused on core CRM automation capabilities. Walked through call recording, email logging, and automatic activity capture in Salesforce. Noah asked several questions about customization and Gong feature parity.

questions_asked.question: Can we customize which fields auto-populate in Salesforce?
questions_asked.answer: Yes, fully configurable field mapping during implementation.

questions_asked.question: Does this include conversation intelligence like Gong's coaching features?
questions_asked.answer: Rep acknowledged Gong's strength there but highlighted broader automation scope.

questions_asked.question: What's most important: call analytics or reducing manual logging?
questions_asked.answer: Noah emphasized time savings for reps as primary driver.

objections: Noah noted Gong has stronger brand recognition in their market

touches.ai_summary: Second demo diving into email automation and workflow features. Demonstrated automated follow-up logging, email tracking, and batch updates. Noah seemed more engaged but continued comparing against Gong's capabilities.

questions_asked.question: How accurate is the email sentiment analysis?
questions_asked.answer: Rep shared accuracy benchmarks and explained AI model training.

questions_asked.question: Are there specific Gong features you consider must-haves?
questions_asked.answer: Noah mentioned deal intelligence dashboard and pipeline visibility.

questions_asked.question: What does unlimited automation tier include vs. base plan?
questions_asked.answer: Rep outlined automation limits across tiers and recommended unlimited for 20 users.

next_steps: Schedule technical eval with Diego present

touches.ai_summary: Technical evaluation with Diego Williams focused on Salesforce API integration, data security, and implementation process. Diego asked detailed questions about SSO, data residency, and API rate limits. Generally positive technical feedback.

questions_asked.question: Do you support SAML SSO with our identity provider?
questions_asked.answer: Yes, supports major SSO providers including Okta, Azure AD.

questions_asked.question: What's your data retention policy and can we configure it?
questions_asked.answer: Rep explained configurable retention with default 24 months, GDPR compliant.

questions_asked.question: Any concerns about the implementation timeline we discussed?
questions_asked.answer: Diego said 3 weeks is workable if team is available for testing.

touches.ai_summary: Executive meeting with CRO Megan Moore to discuss strategic fit and ROI. Rep presented business case showing projected 15 hours/week time savings across sales team. Megan asked about executive reporting and competitive differentiation vs. Gong.

questions_asked.question: What visibility will I have into sales team activity and pipeline health?
questions_asked.answer: Rep demoed executive dashboard with activity metrics and pipeline insights.

questions_asked.question: Why should we choose you over Gong given their market position?
questions_asked.answer: Rep emphasized broader automation scope beyond just calls, better Salesforce integration.

objections: Megan expressed concern about switching costs and change management with sales team
next_steps: Rep to provide executive summary and ROI analysis

touches.ai_summary: Follow-up exec meeting rescheduled by Megan's assistant due to board meeting conflict. Brief coordination to reschedule for following week.

Proposal review call with Noah walking through formal proposal: $16,100 ACV for 20 seats with unlimited automation tier. Noah reacted to pricing and mentioned Gong's competitive quote came in lower. Discussed implementation timeline and support.

questions_asked.question: Can you break down the pricing components?
questions_asked.answer: Rep explained base platform + per-seat + tier pricing structure.

questions_asked.question: What's Gong's pricing looking like for comparison?
questions_asked.answer: Noah indicated Gong quoted ~$13K but with fewer automation features.

questions_asked.question: Is there flexibility on pricing given we're comparing multiple vendors?
questions_asked.answer: Rep said would discuss with leadership given competitive situation.

objections: Pricing higher than Gong's competitive quote

touches.ai_summary: Rep sent follow-up email acknowledging pricing discussion and confirmed working on revised proposal with leadership approval. Reiterated value differentiation vs. Gong on CRM automation breadth.
next_steps: Rep to return with pricing options

touches.ai_summary: Pricing discussion call rescheduled by Noah to allow time for internal Gong evaluation to complete. Noah mentioned they're in final stages with Gong and need to make decision soon.
next_steps: Reschedule pricing call after Gong evaluation concludes

touches.ai_summary: Negotiation call where rep presented revised pricing at $12,200 ACV (24% discount) to remain competitive with Gong while emphasizing superior CRM automation. Noah appreciated the movement but wanted to confirm technical details with Diego before finalizing.

questions_asked.question: Is this your final pricing or is there more room?
questions_asked.answer: Rep confirmed this is approved final pricing, significant discount applied.

questions_asked.question: Can we lock this price for multi-year contract?
questions_asked.answer: Rep offered 2-year lock at this rate with annual payment option.

questions_asked.question: What's the internal decision timeline looking like?
questions_asked.answer: Noah said they need to decide within 2 weeks, waiting on final Gong terms.

objections: Noah mentioned Megan still has concerns about change management and team adoption
next_steps: Rep to send updated proposal with revised pricing
Noah to coordinate final decision meeting

touches.ai_summary: Rep sent updated proposal with $12,200 ACV pricing, 2-year term, and detailed implementation plan. Included change management resources and customer success support plan to address Megan's adoption concerns.
next_steps: Noah to review with Megan and Diego

touches.ai_summary: Final pricing alignment call with Noah and Diego. Walked through revised contract terms, implementation timeline, and success metrics. Noah confirmed technical requirements met and pricing works with budget. Team ready to move forward pending final Megan approval.

questions_asked.question: Can we adjust payment terms to net-45 instead of net-30?
questions_asked.answer: Rep agreed to net-45 payment terms.

questions_asked.question: What's needed to get Megan's final sign-off?
questions_asked.answer: Noah said needs brief exec summary highlighting ROI and competitive positioning.

questions_asked.question: What does the onboarding support look like in first 30 days?
questions_asked.answer: Rep detailed dedicated CSM, weekly check-ins, and technical support hours.

next_steps: Rep to prepare exec summary for Megan
Noah to schedule final approval meeting
